title: haplopappus spinulosus
intro: Haplopappus spinulosus, also known as the prickly goldenweed, is a perennial plant native to the western regions of North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and spiny stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, reducing pain and swelling, and preventing infection. The health benefits of Haplopappus spinulosus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, salve, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
